title: Vellum Systems
compiled_truth:
Vellum Systems is an AI infrastructure startup focused on LLM deployment and fine-tuning for enterprise teams. Founded in 2024 by [Lev Fernandez](people/lev-fernandez), the company has carved out a niche helping mid-to-large organizations manage the lifecycle of large language models — from evaluation and prompt management to production deployment and ongoing fine-tuning. Their platform abstracts away much of the operational complexity that enterprise ML teams face when shipping LLM-powered features, and the pitch has resonated with teams that don't want to build bespoke MLOps pipelines from scratch.

The investor roster includes [Dev Osei](people/dev-osei), [Eli Nakamura](people/eli-nakamura), and [Uri Ramirez](people/uri-ramirez), a mix of angels and early-stage backers who collectively bring experience across infrastructure, enterprise SaaS, and applied AI. Osei in particular has been vocal about the "picks and shovels" thesis in AI — the idea that tooling companies will capture durable value even as the model layer commoditizes. Nakamura came in slightly later but has been an active advisor, especially around go-to-market positioning for techincal buyers.

Vellum's core product is a managed platform that lets engineering teams version, test, and deploy LLM workflows. They support multiple model providers and offer fine-tuning orchestration that handles dataset management, training runs, and evaluation in a unified interface. By late 2027, they had expanded into what they call "model routing" — dynamically selecting which model handles which query based on cost, latency, and accuracy constraints. This feature became a significant differentiator.

Lev Fernandez runs the company with a fairly lean team; headcount hovered around 40 through most of 2027. The engineering culture skews toward shipping fast and iterating with design partners. Vellum caught our attention at Anchor Codec because of their data pipeline requirements — enterprise fine-tuning workflows generate massive volumes of training data and evaluation artifacts, and their team had been exploring deterministic compression approaches for artifact storage and transfer. Early conversations between Vellum and our infra team suggested potential integration points, especially around single-pass data authentication for model weight distribution.

The company's competitive landscape includes several well-funded players, but Vellum has differentiated by focusing specifically on the enterprise segment — SOC 2 compliance, role-based access, audit trails. Their pricing model is consumption-based, which has helped land-and-expand within large accounts. Lev has mentioned publicly that their net revenue retention exceeded 140% in 2027.
timeline:
- **2026-03-12** | Vellum Systems closes a Series A round with participation from [Dev Osei](people/dev-osei) and [Eli Nakamura](people/eli-nakamura), reaching roughly $18M in total funding.
- **2026-07-08** | [Lev Fernandez](people/lev-fernandez) presents at an infrastructure conference on managing LLM deployment complexity at scale; gets positive reception from enterprise attendees.
- **2026-11-15** | Vellum ships fine-tuning orchestration feature, supporting managed training runs across multiple model providers.
- **2027-02-22** | [Uri Ramirez](people/uri-ramirez) joins the cap table as part of a bridge extension; advises on enterprise distribution strategy.
- **2027-05-10** | First exploratory call between Vellum's infra team and Anchor Codec engineering to discuss deterministic compression for training artifact pipelines.
- **2027-08-03** | Vellum launches model routing feature; early design partners report 30% cost reduction on LLM inference workloads.
- **2027-10-19** | Headcount reaches 42. Lev hires a new VP of Engineering from an enterprise data platform background.
- **2028-01-14** | Follow-up meeting with Anchor Codec to scope a potential integration for single-pass authenticated model weight distribution.
- **2028-04-06** | Vellum publicly reports $12M ARR milestone; [Lev Fernandez](people/lev-fernandez) attributes growth to enterprise fine-tuning demand.
- **2028-06-21** | Vellum begins formal pilot with Anchor Codec primitives for secure, compressed artifact transfer in fine-tuning workflows.

_facts.name: Vellum Systems
_facts.description: LLM deployment and fine-tuning infrastructure for enterprise teams
